findings.evidence: Grep hits show the duplicate variants at lines 134, 162, and 192; e.g. line 162 is `cIoo_swap_eq_endpointComplement_sdiff h.symm` after commuting the two erases, and line 192 is just `Nat.add_comm` applied to line 181.
findings.fix: Keep the canonical statements and derive these only at use sites, or reduce them to one-line corollaries using `Finset.erase_right_comm`, `h.symm`, and `Nat.add_comm` if a named lemma is truly needed downstream.
findings.issue: `cIoo_union_swap_eq_erase_erase`, `cIoo_eq_endpointComplement_sdiff_swap`, and `card_cIoo_swap_add_card_cIoo` are duplicate orientation/order variants of the preceding canonical lemmas.

summary: The core `Fin n` complementary-interval facts are at the natural level, but the PR adds several named swapped/order variants instead of deriving them from the canonical statements.
